2606.19625.title: Where Does Social Reasoning Come From? Capability Provenance in Language Models
2606.19625.paper_summary: We use training-data attribution as an interpretable tool for capability discovery, mapping which regions of the pretraining corpus support social-reasoning versus STEM-reasoning in OLMo3-7B. Training-data attribution measures how strongly each training document influences a model's predictions on a benchmark, but document-level scores are too noisy to identify which corpus regions support which capabilities, and prior work has emphasized factual knowledge rather than reasoning. We compute gradient-based attribution (TrackStar via Bergson) over a working set drawn from the de-duplicated Dolma3 mix, aggregate influence across WebOrganizer's 24-format x 24-topic taxonomy (576 bins), and contrast benchmark pairs in a 2x2 design that varies domain (social vs. STEM) and capability type (reasoning vs. knowledge): SocialIQA and MMLU Social Sciences against ARC-Challenge and MMLU STEM. Social and STEM reasoning draw on qualitatively distinct corpus regions, and the contrast is sharper at the reasoning level than at the knowledge level. Targeted machine unlearning provides partial causal validation: forgetting high-attribution topic bins (e.g., Literature for SocialIQA) degrades the aligned benchmark more than within-bin random baselines, and we open-source all code, sampling manifests, the bin-level influence matrix, and unlearning checkpoints.
2606.19625.paper_summary_zh: 我們使用訓練數據歸因作為一種可解釋的工具來發現能力，映射預訓練語料庫中哪些區域支持社會推理與STEM推理在OLMo3-7B中的表現。訓練數據歸因衡量每個訓練文檔對模型在基準測試上預測的影響程度，但文檔級別的分數過於嘈雜，無法識別哪些語料庫區域支持哪些能力，而先前的研究強調了事實知識而非推理。我們計算基於梯度的歸因（通過Bergson的TrackStar）在從去重的Dolma3混合中抽取的工作集上，聚合WebOrganizer的24種格式x 24種主題分類法（576個區間）中的影響，並在一個2x2設計中對比基準對，該設計變化領域（社會與STEM）和能力類型（推理與知識）：SocialIQA和MMLU社會科學對抗ARC-Challenge和MMLU STEM。社會和STEM推理依賴於質量上不同的語料庫區域，並且在推理層面上的對比比在知識層面上更為明顯。針對性機器遺忘提供了部分因果驗證：忘記高歸因主題區間（例如，SocialIQA的文學）對對應基準的影響大於區間內隨機基準，我們開源所有代碼、抽樣清單、區間級影響矩陣和遺忘檢查點。
2606.19625.author: Glenn Matlin et.al.
2606.19625.authors: Glenn Matlin, Chandreyi Chakraborty, Saehee Eom, Mika Okamoto, Rayan Castilla, Louis Jaburi, Alvin Deng, Taywon Min, Lucia Quirke, Stella Biderman, Mark Riedl
